paper_title: Triangle Ramsey numbers of complete graphs
summary: The q-color generalization of Conjecture 1.2 from Fox–Tidor–Zhang (arXiv:2312.06895, published JCTB 176, 2026, pp. 268–286) concerns the q-color analogue of the F-Ramsey number identity $r_{K_3}(K_t) = \binom{r(K_t)}{3}$ for all t. The paper proves the q-color generalizations of its three main theorems (1.1, 1.3, 1.4) by the same arguments, but leaves the q-color analogue of Conjecture 1.2 explicitly open. No follow-up paper resolving this conjecture was found in a wide literature search as of May 2026.

notes: No follow-up found. The paper was published in Journal of Combinatorial Theory Series B 176 (2026) pp. 268–286. Conjecture 1.2 (two-color version) concerns the formula $r_{K_3}(K_t) = \binom{r(K_t)}{3}$ for all t (not just sufficiently large t); the q-color generalization conjectures the analogous identity for q-color F-Ramsey numbers $r_{K_3}^{(q)}(K_t)$. The q-color versions of the proved theorems follow by the same proofs; only the conjecture (requiring a different argument for all t) remains open.
